title: Bridge Essay: Designing for Hope: Biophilic Color Associations and Their Relevance to Clinical

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Ecology/Animism** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Ecology/Animism — biophilia, nature connectedness, environmental neuroscience, neurobiophilia.

**Designing for Hope: Biophilic Color Associations and Their Relevance to Clinical Settings.**. HERD.. PMID: 37194294. DOI: 10.1177/19375867231173410. AIM: To consider one common aspect of biophilic design (BD; i.e., color) and its relationship to an important element of well-being (i.e., hope). BACKGROUND: BD is multifaceted making the identification of critical design elements difficult. Further complexity is introduced given that practice assumptions stemming from the biophilia hypothesis may be questioned.
